title: verbascum × pseudogeorgicum
intro: Verbascum × pseudogeorgicum, also known as the Georgian mullein, is a hybrid plant species derived from the Verbascum genus, which consists of flowering plants native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which contain mucilage and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Verbascum × pseudogeorgicum include soothing irritated skin, reducing inflammation, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its wound-healing and expectorant properties.
